item.dc:creator: Az663

item.title: Move on folks, the show is over. :(
item.dc:creator: LoveThieves

item.title: I still have an odd hobby
item.description:
About 4 years ago I posted about my unusual hobby. My post seemed to go down well so I decided to update it for "Hobby day". I have only once met another person who shares my hobby but there are quite a few of us odd bod out there. I hunt benchmarks. A benchmark is an engraved mark used by mapmakers as part of their map making. Specifically, these marks are used as a fixed point where the cartographers would build a bench on which to site their levelling equipment used to measure the altitude at that point. This is the origin of the word benchmark. These marks were first used in in the 1840s by the Ordnance Survey (the official mapmaking organisation of the UK government) and fell out of use in the 1970’s as the GPS system came into use. The ordnance Survey puts marks on all sorts of structures: - gateposts, walls, houses, churches, castles, 5000-year-old ancient monuments, navigation markers - you name it the OS have probably chiselled their mark on it.
However, these marks are no longer used and are gradually disappearing. Churches get knocked down, extensions get built obscuring the mark, bridges get widened, gatepost are pulled up – these marks are disappearing. So why would I want to hunt down these humble marks? Why not? I enjoy scouring over the old maps. These are things of beauty – meticulously researched, drawn and printed.
Section of the 1895 OS map of Stockton-on-Tees with three different marks shown (the little arrows with BM and the altitude) Marks in town can be easy to find – but also frequently destroyed. I hunt for the more obscure marks, preferably out in the wilds.
No nearby features to help with the hunt for this mark. There is the challenge of the calculation of the coordinates of the mark (no easy feat as the old maps do not have an easy-to-use coordinate system). I need to worry about the accuracy of the map makers, the accuracy of my calculations and, when in the field, the accuracy of my GPS. The cumulative errors can lead me to needing to search 10,000 square feet.
Where is the mark in these boulders? Then there is the challenge of the journey. The marks are rarely on waymarked paths, so I need to trek across sometimes ankle turning marshland, ford streams that have taken on the proportions of cataracts due to the rainfall and navigate through wind, rain, snow, mist and sometimes even sunshine.
Four times in five I have no joy. The marks have been lost to time. Grass or heather has overgrown the mark, streams have washed the boulders away wind, snow and rain have eroded the mark, my coordinates may have been wrong – who knows. But – oh – that fifth time. Finding the chiselled mark, turning over the sod to find the mark not noticed for 170 years, last used by Victorian map makers or turning my head to catch the faint shadow of that mark now almost eroded. Well, I tell you, I do the dance. I don’t worry about how I look – I’m almost always on my own with not a soul in sight anyway. I record the position, photograph it from different angles and then trudge across the moor for the next hunt. Then back home to type up my finding, post the cords online and edit the photos (and post them on Imgur of course). I do not know how many people will have started reading this, just to lose interest half way through. How many have got this far and thought I must be a definite oddball? But I don’t care. I’m harming no one, keeping mentally and physically active, seeing the best of British countryside and enjoying myself, all the time helping to record a fascinating, but obscure part of cartographic history. Repost edit I am still hunting these marks. I have looked for over 10,000 of them (and found a respectable 4250) I was out hunting marks in Newcastle upon Tyne yesterday and today I was on the hills near the border between England and Scotland.
A mark that dates from 1950 - a new one for me.
This mark dates from 1861. It is the type and age of mark that gives me great pleasure to find. I like to think that I am the first person in 160 years to look for this mark, chiselled into a boulder on the middle of the moor but the cartographers of old. This is the sign of a good day.
item.dc:creator: WeTRyehard

item.title: Trans Girl Tuesday
item.description:
Wanted to do another timeline post. These first few were from the beginning of June, for Pride! There was a reply chain with several trans women sharing their before and afters, and it was really cute. HRT is literally magic.
...this was just a joke, lol. Although she said she did get compared to Dylan & Cole when she was younger.
item.dc:creator: MermaidNinja

item.title: Anyone else?
item.description:
It's weird, because she also told him that he should expect to be sleepy and maybe a little worn out because of things coming up that he hasn't thought about for decades. Turns out she was right.
Chessie loves it when her cat-dad takes a nap on the couch.

item.title: Boyfriend surprises his girlfriend with a wonderful reunion with her parents after being apart for so long
item.dc:creator: Hariongsta

item.title: Brumation of snapping turtle
item.description: "Brumation, the term for a reptile's dormant state during cold months, is a natural survival mechanism for many species, including snapping turtles. Unlike true hibernation, which is seen in warm-blooded animals, brumation allows reptiles to significantly slow their metabolic processes while remaining somewhat reactive to their surroundings." Source: https://www.newsweek.com/turtle-hibernating-brumation-frozen-lake-viral-tiktok-1996221
item.dc:creator: Fulustreka

item.title: First time catching my cake day. Hard to choose a favorite for 11 years, but I'm on my way to run the LGBTQ+ Scout Meeting I Scoutmaster for so this is the most appropriate.
item.description: In case you're wondering, the LGBTQ+ kids in my troop do crazy LGBTQ+ kid things like camp, hike, raft, community service projects, go to the beach and make fun of Nazis.

item.title: Big Jack may be dangerous on demand, but he’s not without compassion.
item.description: Source is @petfoolery on instagram. MV edit: thanks for the fake internet points my friends. For those equating this with the CEO shooter, user @acinonyxjubatus pointed out that this was actually created several days before that. It was also pointed out that this is very in keeping with Big Jack’s character. So I’m just gonna try to appreciate a good comic and quality OC for what it is. Stay safe y’all.

item.dc:creator: kamelj436

item.title: My hobby!
item.description:
In February 2019, I decided to buy some paint and canvases, and paint with good old Bob Ross. I started painting to help with some major medical related anxiety and panic attacks after losing my father to cancer. I’m so glad that I did. I hope you all enjoy. The last painting was that very first! I hated it :D Most viral edit (!?): to check out more of my work and follow along my painting journey, follow my instagram at www.instagram.com/paintings_by_adam_stevens
My first ever painting!
item.dc:creator: PilotBuilder

item.title: The DnD tracker is finally complete!
item.description: Hi everyone! After a lot of work (and even starting from scratch due to a software error), the tracker board is finally finished and ready to help you stay organized during your games! It includes: - HP & Temp HP - Spell Save DC & Spell Attack Modifier - Spell Slots - Death Saves - Conditions - Character Level - Customizable Miscellaneous Trackers (coins, consumables, AC, etc.) The magnetic sliders snap into place with a satisfying click, and you can personalize the board for your character. Get it here: To print your own, you can download the files for free on my Patreon: https://www.patreon.com/posts/tracker-is-117641346 There's no need to register or pledge unless you actually want to support my work. Everything I make is free for everyone. Though you can join as a free member to receive updates. If you don't have a 3D printer or don't want to print your own, I also sell them on my Etsy store: https://www.etsy.com/dk-en/listing/1825915152/dnd-player-tracker-board-all-in-one Thank you for all your support! Now that the tracker is done, I’ll start working on incorporating it into the full DnD game box. Stay tuned for updates! DM Daniel ?‍♂️

item.title: The human body ready for plastination
item.description: "Plastination is a process designed to preserve the body for educational and instructional purposes – in a more detailed way than ever before. Plastinates are dry, odorless, durable and are particularly valuable educational tools not only for medical professionals but also for a broader public." Source: https://bodyworlds.com/plastination/plastination-technique/
item.dc:creator: Fulustreka

item.title: Who's left?!?!
item.dc:creator: ComradeBinch

item.title: Thousands of Federal employees scared of losing their jobs after criticizing Trump
item.description: Federal employees are scrubbing their social media accounts of any negative posts about President-elect Donald Trump as they scramble to save their jobs before his inauguration. Thousands of civil servants are taking steps to protect themselves from plans to slash the number of federal employees promised by Trump and his allies, according to The Washington Post. Some federal employees are removing posts from X and Facebook, with one official who testified in Trump’s first impeachment inquiry considering filing retirement papers as others are taking steps to move to possibly safer agencies. Recruiting firms in the nation’s capital have seen an increase in business from those looking for work in the private sector. Some agencies have taken action to reclassify some jobs with titles that go against the Trump agenda, such as those including Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion, environmental justice, and those looking to address the climate crisis. Some civil servants are taking out liability insurance for the first time in case they need attorneys if they’re demoted or fired. Departing Biden administration appointees are working with labor unions to extend collective bargaining agreements, securing benefits before the second Trump administration can take action to remove them. Staffers are working to take steps to protect themselves ahead of Trump instituting his plans to reshape the 2.3 million-strong bureaucracy. Trump has said he plans to fire thousands of federal employees and replace them with allies, and he intends to cut the federal budget by trillions, partly by fully shutting down some departments and relocating others. Trump has said he wants to move some agencies away from Washington, D.C., and its federal workforce. https://www.yahoo.com/news/federal-employees-scrub-social-media-155558426.html
item.dc:creator: puzzledeagle

item.title: May the Galactic Federation help you
item.dc:creator: SkyPigeon123

item.title: Meme dump of cat memes that made me laugh.
item.description:
I got my buddy Baxter 3 and a half years ago. Barn cat, took him home when he was 6 weeks old.
I got Baxter when my cat Flake was 13 years old. Flake hated having a kitten around.
Baxter was a true orange, he always seemed to almost have the braincell that day.
Bax HATED the vet.
The whole neighborhood knew him. Bax secretly had 3 other families. Always taken care of. His collar had “Not a stray, I have a home” on his tag with our numbers in case something happened.
Baxter was the most feral/domesticated cat I’ve had the pleasure of living with. He’d act like a wild beast, and then drool on your chest from being so happy with the scritches and cuddles.
Bax passed over the rainbow bridge this morning…
He loved being outside, but was inside every night to lay at my feet in bed and for cuddles.
He was a barn cat that we had to bottle feed when we first got him because he was so young. He was 100% feral at first.
Cat Taxes. Baxter and Luna.
How he greeted me when I came home.
Such a little menace. He grew to the size of a mid sized dog and about 20 pounds of pure muscle.
I miss you buddy…he passed away on our way to the vet this morning for an emergency intake. Yesterday he was fine and acting a little strange come night time. This morning we woke up and he was making strange noises and wouldn’t move. The vet said he passed from a UTI that passed into his bladder and enlarged it. And “unfortunately” by the time cats begin to display signs something is wrong…it’s already too late. I can’t get over the fact that he was only 3 and a half…I want more time with my little dude…fuck..
item.dc:creator: P0sit1veTh0ughts

item.title: Fresh(ish) Memes #405
item.dc:creator: ShaggyRoots

item.title: Memes vol 441
item.description:
When you post mean comments- this is who’s reading them
AARON TVEIT MENTIONED BWAHHHH I don’t actually like Les mis though https://youtu.be/1ikqU6G6Xgs?si=418uqUlLXM3ISfYZ
Now get ready for: my favorite tweets regarding Luigi Mangione
NO BETA WE DIE LIKE BRIAN THOMPSON IS TAKING ME OUT
Oh- here’s my Christmas tree I guess. It’s so cute I’m keeping it in here
I had to save this because my boyfriends little sister ate my burrito that I had already bit into (in an effort to keep somebody from stealing it)
Deny Defend Depose
Ahhh chemicals and salt. Also this looks like my boyfriend as a little kid (it’s literally just the hair)
Liza Jane and Scruffy tax (notice the wicked reference)
Froggy tax
Nessie tax. This dump was kind of all over the place and I apologize but I was throwing up 2 hours ago and am still kind of out of it. https://imgur.com/gallery/9yD5Ghn
